other_observers: Jason St. Pierre & Amy Dobbins arrived in about 1/2 hour.  Ryan Votta & Jim Beatty arrived about 1:00 PM.
description: This warbler had a yellow head, except for dark smudgy marks on the cheek and dark gray that extended from the back of head to the crown.  (Forehead was yellow.)  Bill was pointed.   Chin and throat were white, but there was a very faint dusky patch below the throat.  (Because this dusky patch was not seen in all views, I wonder if this appearance was due to dark bases of those feathers.)  Back appeared mostly gray (with perhaps a hint of olive) and with sparse, diffuse streaks.  The bird had gray wings with 2 distinct white wing bars (with the upper appearing wider).  The upperside of the tail was gray with extensive white to the outer tail feathers.  Underparts were white, except there were a few sparse, dark streaks on either side of the chest.   The vent was white.  The absence of a black throat indicated a first-fall bird.  On review of Jim Beatty's photos, the dark centers of the median coverts also supported aging the bird as a first-fall individual.
